title: 0968_www.singaporetech.edu.sg_undergraduate-programmes_robotics-systems.html
text: Home Academics Robotics Systems Robotics Systems A specialised engineering programme that focuses on robotics and systems engineering Programme Overview Qualification BEng (Hons) Provider Singapore Institute of Technology Duration 4 years Total credits 240 Application period 8 January to 19 March 2025 Next intake 01 September 2025 Campus location SIT Punggol Campus The Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Robotics Systems is a four-year, direct honours degree programme that focuses on robotics and systems engineering. The programme aims to fulfil the anticipated demand for robotics engineers, as robotic systems increases their services to their human counterparts in various applications. The integration of multiple engineering disciplines, via systems engineering through its project-based pedagogy, is a unique feature of the programme. Software and Artificial Intelligence (AI) are used as integrative elements that connect multiple mechatronics elements together to form a complete pedagogy, is a unique feature of the programme. Software and Artificial Intelligence (AI) are used as integrative elements that connect multiple mechatronics elements together to form a complete robotic system. The Robotics Systems curriculum is centred around the design and development of deployable robotic systems. Robotics modules deal with the actual design and development of mechatronics sub-systems, while systems engineering modules look at the efficient execution of the robotics systems development process. Software development modules integrate software/firmware sub-systems, including artificial intelligence algorithms, to generate intelligent behaviours. A significant amount of project-based learning activities that connect academic knowledge and skills with real-world applications has been incorporated into the programme. These projects allow students to simultaneously experience systems and software engineering, project management, as well as systemic integration of has been incorporated into the programme. These projects allow students to simultaneously experience systems and software engineering, project management, as well as systemic integration of knowledge from multiple disciplines. The 12-month Integrated Work Study Programme and the capstone project will serve as the culmination and integrative experience of the programme. Programme Educational Objectives Graduates of the programme, after working three to five years, are expected to: Develop and implement complex, lifecycle-oriented solutions involving robotic systems. Demonstrate continuous growth in subject matter expertise within the field of engineering and beyond. Successfully lead or significantly contribute to trans-disciplinary teams on complex projects (e.g. engineering projects). Integrate ethical considerations and societal impact into their professional practice. Curriculum Highlights Robotics Development Systems Engineering Project Management Artificial Intelligence and Integrate ethical considerations and societal impact into their professional practice. Curriculum Highlights Robotics Development Systems Engineering Project Management Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Software Engineering Mechatronics 12-month Integrated Work Study Programme Overseas Immersion Programme Remote video URL Industry Partnership With the aim of growing graduates highly valued by the industry, a tight nexus has been forged between the curriculum offered by SIT and the specific skills needed by the industry. SIT works closely with our industry partners to ensure our students develop professional competencies through the Integrated Work Study Programme (IWSP), industry capstone projects, and applied research projects. Partnering Siemens Pte Ltd, SIT developed a new degree specialisation in the Robotics Systems programme. The degree specialisation will deepen graduates’ knowledge and skills in Industry 4.0 technologies for the advanced manufacturing sector, such degree specialisation in the Robotics Systems programme. The degree specialisation will deepen graduates’ knowledge and skills in Industry 4.0 technologies for the advanced manufacturing sector, such as robotics and artificial intelligence (AI). Remote video URL Integrated Work Study Programme Integrated Work Study Programme (IWSP) provides students with the opportunity to undertake real work. Held over the span of 12 months, it is designed to be more in-depth than a traditional internship. You will gain real work experience and apply the theories you learn in class with actual practice. Overseas Immersion Programme (OIP) As part of the curriculum, students can participate in a two-week Overseas Immersion Programme at Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU), where they will attend courses and hands-on workshops on embodied AI and robotics. Hosted at SJTU’s Student Innovation Centre, the programme exposes students to cutting-edge research in robotic agility, intelligence, and courses and hands-on workshops on embodied AI and robotics. Hosted at SJTU’s Student Innovation Centre, the programme exposes students to cutting-edge research in robotic agility, intelligence, and human-machine interaction, with applications spanning logistics, healthcare, and smart cities. Beyond technical learning, the immersion also offers valuable cross-cultural experiences, giving students international exposure and insights into global industry practices that prepare them to contribute to Singapore’s future robotics and AI ecosystem. Term-In Term-Out (TITO) The TITO pathway allows students to alternate their time between the university and the workplace every year. At the end of their full-time undergraduate studies, students on this pathway would have spent extended time learning and immersing in authentic industry settings to gain substantial work experience without prolonging the duration of their degree programme. The TITO pathway exemplifies the foundation of our applied and immersing in authentic industry settings to gain substantial work experience without prolonging the duration of their degree programme. The TITO pathway exemplifies the foundation of our applied learning pedagogy, which is ‘learning by doing’, and we believe that this will give students a stronger head-start to the working world by exposing them to the latest industry developments, business intricacies and work cultures. text: Home Academics Speech and Language Therapy Speech and Language Therapy Helping individuals with communication and/or swallowing difficulties. Programme Overview Qualification BSc (Hons) Provider Singapore Institute of Technology Duration 4 years Total credits 240 Application period 8 January to 19 March 2025 Next intake 01 September 2025 Campus location SIT Punggol Campus The Bachelor of Science with Honours in Speech and Language Therapy is a four-year, direct honours degree programme, developed with speech and language therapy practitioners from the public healthcare, community care, social services and private sectors. The Speech and Language Therapy undergraduate degree programme in Singapore has been carefully developed with senior speech and language therapy practitioners from the local public healthcare, community care, social services, and private sectors. As the first undergraduate speech and language therapy degree programme in Singapore, the programme serves to meet the local public healthcare, community care, social services, and private sectors. As the first undergraduate speech and language therapy degree programme in Singapore, the programme serves to meet the national needs in preparing a new generation of highly-competent and industry-ready speech and language therapists who will promote communication, feeding and swallowing health for people across the lifespan. The programme has obtained accreditation from the Allied Health Professions Council (AHPC), thus enabling graduates to practise as professional Speech-Language Therapists in Singapore. Do note that the healthcare staff guidelines and requirements that apply to healthcare institutions also apply to Health Sciences students. Non-compliance with these guidelines may affect your ability in meeting course requirements, graduating, obtaining registration or employment, and fulfilling bond obligations. It is crucial to adhere to these guidelines to ensure successful completion of your studies course requirements, graduating, obtaining registration or employment, and fulfilling bond obligations. It is crucial to adhere to these guidelines to ensure successful completion of your studies and future professional opportunities in the healthcare field. Curriculum Highlights Communication Development & Disorders in Preschool and School-age Children Acquired Cognitive-communication and Language Disorders Speech Disorders in Children and Adults Augmentative and Alternative Communication Fluency and Voice Disorders across the Lifespan Feeding and Swallowing Disorders in Children and Adults Clinical Fieldwork and Interdisplinary Workshops 29 weeks of Clinical Practice Education Clinical Practice Education All students need to undergo approximately 1000 hours of clinical practice education (CPE), including clinical skills classes and placements across various clinical settings. During CPE, students learn to apply their academic and theoretical knowledge to real-world clinical education (CPE), including clinical skills classes and placements across various clinical settings. During CPE, students learn to apply their academic and theoretical knowledge to real-world clinical practice. With progressive clinical exposure and guidance from practising clinicians, students build confidence in their clinical skills. Students need to pass all clinical placements to graduate from the programme and to be registered under the Allied Health Professions Council (AHPC). Students may be eligible for an exit award "Bachelor of Science in Care Sciences" if they fail to meet clinical practice education module requirements for the award of an honours degree, subject to fulfilling conditions for the exit award. Career Options Graduates can be employed as speech and language therapists in a variety of settings, such as: Child Development Centres Special Schools Mainstream School Services Acute Hospitals text: Authority, Singapore. The direct honours degree programme provides mechanical engineering training with specialisation electives in (i) Integrated Facility Management (ii) Green Building Design and (iii) Agritech and Aquaculture to prepare you to become a building mechanical engineer. Graduates will also develop lifelong skills to ensure they stay relevant in the Built Environment sector which focus much on decarbonization, energy efficiency and Sustainable Urban System. Students will go through rigorous academic training conducted by highly qualified professors and professional officers, as well as a eight-month Integrated Work Study Programme in the industry. SBE students are being trained to be eligible for Professional Engineering (Mechanical) qualification. Students who perform well in the programme may proceed to pursue the Master of Science in Mechanical Engineering programme with Specialisation in Sustainable Building Technology. Programme Educational Objectives Graduates of in the programme may proceed to pursue the Master of Science in Mechanical Engineering programme with Specialisation in Sustainable Building Technology. Programme Educational Objectives Graduates of the Master of Science (Mechanical Engineering) with Bachelor of Engineering in Sustainable Built Environment (SBE) programmes, after working for three to five years, are expected to be: Competent in solving complex engineering issues in the Built Environment with critical thinking and skillsets in Sustainability and Integrated Digital Technologies. Nimble to embrace challenges and opportunities by adapting to industry transformation of the Built environment through lifelong professional development Effective in communicating, leading and nurturing teams to transform the Built Environment with strong grounding at work processes and system levels. Contribute in advancing the Built Environment community with due consideration of health, safety, social, productivity and environmental aspects. strong grounding at work processes and system levels. title: 0973_www.singaporetech.edu.sg_undergraduate-programmes_user-experience-and-game-design.html
text: Home Academics User Experience and Game Design User Experience and Game Design Create meaningful and engaging interactive experiences. Programme Overview Qualification Bachelor of Arts Provider DigiPen Institute of Technology Singapore Duration 4 years Total credits 240 Application period 8 January to 19 March 2025 Next intake 01 September 2025 Campus location SIT Punggol Campus The Bachelor of Arts in User Experience and Game Design is a multidisciplinary degree that combines coursework in user experience design theory, the humanities, and social sciences to equip you with the skills to create meaningful and engaging interactive experiences. As a student, you will be enriched with the technical and aesthetic principles that make interactive experiences intuitive and engaging, as well as the tools and processes that designers use to implement, test and refine their ideas in a real-world production environment. You will become a highly skilled design expert, capable of merging writing, tools and processes that designers use to implement, test and refine their ideas in a real-world production environment. You will become a highly skilled design expert, capable of merging writing, visual communication and the social sciences into stunning games, user interfaces and other interactive experiences. Curriculum The programme focuses on the following subjects: Game design and development, including game mechanics and history, design documentation, scripting and programming, 2D and 3D level design, character design, playtesting, interface design, product management, and team game project implementation. The humanities and arts, including writing, psychology, film, audio, architecture, drawing, and 2D and 3D art, with the option to study advanced topics in these areas. Foundational math and science courses, including the fundamentals of scripting languages, mathematics, and physics. Learn more about the Bachelor of Arts in User Experience and Game Design programme offered by math and science courses, including the fundamentals of scripting languages, mathematics, and physics. Learn more about the Bachelor of Arts in User Experience and Game Design programme offered by DigiPen (Singapore) . Internship Students can choose to opt for an Internship in their 10th and 11th trimesters. An internship is any carefully monitored work or service experience in which an individual has intentional learning goals and reflects actively on what she or he is learning throughout the experience. It is usually a professional activity under general supervision of an experienced professional and in a job situation, which places a high degree of responsibility on the student. Overseas Immersion Programme The Overseas Immersion Programme (OIP) is mandatory for students in the BA in User Experience and Game Design program. Students will study for one trimester at DigiPen Institute of Technology’s U.S. campus in Redmond, Washington, attending lectures, labs, and industry seminars. Experience and Game Design program. Students will study for one trimester at DigiPen Institute of Technology’s U.S. campus in Redmond, Washington, attending lectures, labs, and industry seminars. Students will interact with American professors and mentors while experiencing life in a different culture with their overseas peers. title: 0974_www.singaporetech.edu.sg_whistleblowing.html
text: Home SIT Whistleblowing Channels SIT Whistleblowing Channels Overview SIT strives towards the highest standards of governance, ethics and conduct. The SIT Whistleblowing Channels provide individuals with avenues to report, in good faith, any wrongful practices in SIT, without fear of reprisal against the whistleblower. Wrongful practices include, but are not limited to: • Inappropriate or unethical behaviour; • Academic or professional malpractice; • Misappropriation or misuse of SIT assets; • Endangerment of the health and safety of persons; • Violation of laws and regulations; • Fraud, corruption or bribery; • Concealment of information relating to wrongful practices. Make a report Whistleblowing reports can be sent to the following: By Email Whistleblow@SingaporeTech.edu.sg By Post 1 Punggol Coast Road, Singapore 828608 (Addressed to “Director, Internal Audit” in a sealed envelope marked “Confidential”) Whistleblowing reports on the Internal Audit Division should be directed to the Coast Road, Singapore 828608 (Addressed to “Director, Internal Audit” in a sealed envelope marked “Confidential”) Whistleblowing reports on the Internal Audit Division should be directed to the President, SIT. Information to be provided • Description of the wrongful practice, including where and when it occurred; • Names of the persons involved, and their job appointments or roles; • Details of any witnesses or evidence; • Any other information that will aid in the subsequent investigation; • Contact details to facilitate any clarification required by SIT. Confidentiality of information provided While we can understand that some individuals may wish to remain anonymous, reports made anonymously may lack credibility or hinder investigations. Therefore, we encourage whistleblowers to identify themselves to facilitate the investigation. The identity of the whistleblower and the information provided will be protected but may, under certain circumstances (e.g. where it is legally required themselves to facilitate the investigation. The identity of the whistleblower and the information provided will be protected but may, under certain circumstances (e.g. where it is legally required among other reasons), be disclosed on a need-to-know basis to relevant parties. SIT does not condone any acts of reprisal against a whistleblower. Any alleged acts of reprisal will be investigated promptly and action taken against any person found to have committed such acts.

title: 0975_www.singaporetech.edu.sg_why-sit_applied-learning.html
text: Home Why SIT Applied Learning Applied Learning At SIT, we define Applied Learning as our pedagogy of choice, as it combines academic knowledge and skills with real-world applications in the industry and community. We distinguish ourselves by pioneering and championing a distinct applied degree pathway with a close nexus with industry. Our Applied Learning framework fosters the development of in-depth, domain-specific knowledge, industry readiness and transferable skills in our graduates, integrating learning, industry, and community. Student-Centred Approach to Applied Learning Applied learning requires active learning. To help our students learn actively, we have adopted a range of teaching methodologies, such as experiential learning, flipped classrooms, and problem-based learning with a real-life context. Our curriculum and programmes are designed and developed by our academic staff, who are experts in their respective fields. They play a key role in facilitating students’ learning real-life context. Our curriculum and programmes are designed and developed by our academic staff, who are experts in their respective fields. They play a key role in facilitating students’ learning by providing the industry and community context. To ensure that the curriculum remains relevant to evolving industry needs, we conduct regular consultation and collaboration with the industry. As part of their degree programmes, students undergo the Integrated Work Study Programme (IWSP), in which they are immersed in the real work environment of the industry for six to 12 months, integrating what they have learnt in the classroom with industry practice. They are supported by Professional Officers with extensive industry experience, who act as coaches and mentors to students undergoing IWSP, in both their project work and career development. Finally, peers also play an important role in applied learning. They serve as a support system by offering constructive and critical feedback to help both their project work and career development. title: 0976_www.singaporetech.edu.sg_why-sit_our-philosophy.html
text: Home Why SIT Our Education Philosophy Our Education Philosophy Delivering an education that truly adds value and develops individuals by building on their interests and talents. An Education That Truly Adds Value At SIT, we believe in developing you by building on your interests and talents. Your education experience with SIT will prepare you to be an effective professional and give you the agility and preparedness to thrive in your career in this era of disruption and emerging opportunities. Attainment of Disciplinary Knowledge, Transferable Skills and Interdisciplinarity Our curriculum is imbued with interdisciplinarity. Students will acquire a broad suite of skills and knowledge from their majors and specialisations as well as adjacent disciplines, that are highly relevant in the industry. Along with a firm grounding in transferable skills gained from within and outside of formal learning, we want to develop you to be a versatile and effective professional in the workplace. Career Along with a firm grounding in transferable skills gained from within and outside of formal learning, we want to develop you to be a versatile and effective professional in the workplace. Career Preparation and Personal Growth Beyond our formal curriculum, we will equip you with the necessary career skills to succeed in the workplace. Our vibrant campus community offers a wide range of extracurricular activities that will enable you to forge lifelong friendships, expand your experiences, and help you grow as an individual and as a leader. Embodiment of SITizen-DNA From day one, we want to cultivate the SITizen-DNA in you, i.e., to be a Thinking Tinkerer, who is Able to Learn, Unlearn and Relearn, and to be a Catalyst for Transformation while staying Grounded in the Community. We want you to have an exceptional experience in SIT, begin a lifelong relationship with SIT as you excel in your career, grow, and learn to be a lifelong learner.

title: 0978_www.singaporetech.edu.sg_why-sit_university-programme-structure_irsf.html
text: Home Why SIT University Programme Structure Industry-Ready Skills Framework (IRSF) Industry-Ready Skills Framework (IRSF) Truly Industry-Ready At SIT, we prepare students holistically for the world of work. Through the Industry-Ready Skills Framework (IRSF) , our students can plan and develop essential skills in a structured manner. Upon graduation, every student will receive a Record of Transferable Skills (RTS) that complements their academic transcript. RTS is a documentation of the transferable skills achieved during a student's time at SIT. It serves as a conversation starter with potential employers about the skills an SIT graduate can bring beyond academic qualifications. Figure 1: Industry-Ready Skills Framework (IRSF) Implemented in 2021 1 , the IRSF allows students to map skills in the following areas: Disciplinary Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities : Core applied degree learning outcomes. Transferable Skills : Critical core skills that are considered workplace essentials by in the following areas: Disciplinary Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities : Core applied degree learning outcomes. Transferable Skills : Critical core skills that are considered workplace essentials by employers and industry, categorised into 5 skill areas 2 – Thinking Agility, People Agility, Digital Agility, Professional Agility, and Change Agility (see figure 2). SITizen-DNA Attributes : Qualities that help students be resilient and adaptable in a changing workplace. Figure 2: IRSF – 5 skills areas The framework helps students understand the skills they will develop and demonstrate from the roles and activities they take up at SIT. How can I use it as a Student or Employer? The IRSF incorporates 18 transferable skills across 5 skill areas. This suite of skills was identified as essential for workplace success, drawing on feedback from employers and industry leaders. The IRSF is also adapted from SkillsFuture Singapore's Critical Core Skills . Each student’s skill achievements are for workplace success, drawing on feedback from employers and industry leaders. The IRSF is also adapted from SkillsFuture Singapore's Critical Core Skills . Each student’s skill achievements are mapped onto the RTS, which indicates their mastery of different skill areas across these levels: "emerging," "proficient," and "highly proficient." The RTS transcript serves as a showcase of a student’s transferable skills developed both within and beyond their academic curriculum. With the RTS, both students and employers can identify the key strengths and competencies of each SITizen, which can be matched to an organisation’s needs. Figure 3: A sample of the RTS transcript: The radar chart (left) shows the average score of all skills under each skill area. The table (right) lists the 18 transferable skills attained by level of proficiency, ranging from emerging to proficient to highly proficient. More Information: Have a burning question on the IRSF and RTS and their benefits for job skills attained by level of proficiency, ranging from emerging to proficient to highly proficient. More Information: Have a burning question on the IRSF and RTS and their benefits for job placement and skills development? All students will have a basic radar chart curriculum refers to the academic modules that provide students with the disciplinary knowledge and skills needed for their applied degree programmes. All students will have a basic radar chart tailored to their programme of study. 4 Outside curriculum refers to programmes, activities and experiences organised by SIT that help students develop specific skills and attributes through their roles (e.g., executive committee member, facilitator, participant).

title: SIT Degree
text: for computer science is it SIT only degree?No, the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) is not the only provider of computer science degrees, but it offers several specialized computer science programs. For example, SIT offers a joint degree with the University of Glasgow and a specialized degree in Computing Science with a focus on Internet of Things (IoT). Other providers, like DigiPen Institute of Technology Singapore, also offer computer science degrees, some of which are in partnership with SIT. SIT and its partners BSc (Hons) Computing Science (with University of Glasgow): This is a 3-year joint degree with a focus on IoT, software engineering, data analytics, and cybersecurity, according to the Singapore Institute of Technology. BSc (Hons) in Computer Science in Real-Time Interactive Simulation (with DigiPen): A 4-year degree focused on creating complex software systems and is offered jointly with DigiPen Institute of Technology Singapore. BSc (Hons) Computer Science in Interactive Media and Game Development (with DigiPen): Another 4-year joint degree with DigiPen, specializing in interactive media and game development.

title: SIT Food court
text: Is there any student discount at food court in SIT?Yes, SIT students and staff receive a 10% discount at the on-campus food court, Foodgle Hub, and potentially at some other specific F&B vendors in the vicinity, by presenting their student ID. SIT Food Court Discounts Foodgle Hub (SIT Punggol Campus): Students can register using their SIT email to get a 10% discount on meals at Foogle Hub, the main food court on the Punggol campus. Some sources indicate staff also get this discount. Kopifellas (Punggol Coast Mall): SIT students and staff get 10% off at the Kopifellas outlet in Punggol Coast Mall (which is nearby the campus) when flashing their ID. General Information The food court aims to provide affordable options, with most meals priced between $4 to $8, which is generally considered budget-friendly for students even before the discount. The food court is open to SIT students and staff during restricted hours (e.g., 11:30 am to 2 pm on weekdays), with public access available outside of those times. Specific promotions may vary by stall and are subject to change. It is always best to present your student card and check with individual vendors for the latest deals.

title: SIT Medical leave
text: Do i have to take MC (medical certificate) for absence? Yes, you must submit a Medical Certificate (MC) for leave to be officially recognized, whether it's for a job or a school. While you must inform your employer or institution within 48 hours, you are typically required to submit the MC when you return to work or apply for the leave through the designated online portal. For employmentNotify your employer: Inform your employer as soon as possible, and at most within 48 hours, of your absence from work due to illness. Submit the MC: Submit the MC to your employer when you return to work as proof of your sick leave.Digital MCs: If your MC is digital, you may be able to submit it directly through an online portal. Ensure the digital MC is from a valid domain like mc.gov.sg and includes the doctor's information. For educational institutionsFollow institutional procedures: Each institution has its own process. For example, Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) requires students to apply for short leave via their online portal (IN4SIT) and submit materials via SRS within seven days of the medical certificate being issued.Notify the instructor: It is also best practice to notify your instructor directly via email about your absence, as stated in the DigiPen (Singapore) attendance policy.Meet deadlines: Be sure to meet any specific deadlines, such as submitting your MC within 24 hours of the missed class if it's the final week of the trimester.

title: SIT campus
text: Can we still visit SIT dover campus?No, students cannot move to the Dover campus because SIT has completed its move to the new centralized campus in Punggol. The Dover campus was the former headquarters and has been returned to the authorities, and the last of its programs moved on May 5, 2025. Former SIT campus: SIT's former headquarters was at 10 Dover Drive. New central campus: All of SIT's campuses and corporate offices have now moved to the new Punggol Campus in the Punggol Digital District. Punggol Campus: This new, single campus is designed to unify the SIT community and foster collaboration with industry partners. Dover campus status: The Dover site is no longer used by SIT and will eventually be demolished to make way for new housing developments. Campus Map and Navigation Information: If you need help finding your way around the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT), you can use the official SIT Campus Wayfinder. It provides an interactive map that shows building locations, teaching blocks, food courts, labs, and key facilities across the SIT@Punggol campus. You can search for lecture theatres, classrooms, sports facilities, student services, and other points of interest. The tool helps students, staff, and visitors locate venues for events, directions between buildings, and nearest amenities such as ATMs or bus stops. Explore the full interactive campus map here: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/campus-wayfinder. Note: SIT has fully moved to the Punggol Campus within the Punggol Digital District. The former Dover campus is no longer in use.

title: SIT campus area
text:
SIT Campus Locations and Buildings Information:
The Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) has transitioned from multiple satellite campuses to a single centralized campus in Punggol. Below are the current, former, and partner campus details.

Current Campus:
1. SIT Punggol Campus (Main Address) – Located at 1 Punggol Coast Road, Singapore 828608. This is the main and centralized SIT campus beside Punggol Coast MRT station. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/9ZpZ5o2kY4Q2QvKZA)
2. SIT Campus Heart – Address: 1 Punggol Coast Road, Singapore 828608. This core section of the Punggol campus includes four academic buildings, the Ngee Ann Kongsi Library, and Makerspace. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/9ZpZ5o2kY4Q2QvKZA)
3. SIT Campus Court – Address: 1 Punggol Coast Road, Singapore 828608. This section includes six buildings such as the Kwek Leng Beng University Tower, Ho Bee Auditorium, and Foodgle Hub. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/9ZpZ5o2kY4Q2QvKZA)

Buildings within the Punggol Campus:
4. Kwek Leng Beng University Tower – Located within Campus Court. Houses administrative offices and teaching facilities. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/9ZpZ5o2kY4Q2QvKZA)
5. Ho Bee Auditorium – Located within Campus Court. A large 800-seat auditorium used for events and lectures. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/9ZpZ5o2kY4Q2QvKZA)
6. Ngee Ann Kongsi Library – Located within Campus Heart. The main library facility at SIT Punggol. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/9ZpZ5o2kY4Q2QvKZA)
7. SIT Visitor Gallery – Located at Level 2 and below (public access zone). Serves as the public-facing area of the campus with exhibits and outreach spaces. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/9ZpZ5o2kY4Q2QvKZA)

Food Courts and Dining:
8. Foodgle Hub – Located at SIT Campus Court, Block E4. Main food court offering a variety of local and international stalls. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/9ZpZ5o2kY4Q2QvKZA)
9. Wholesome by Food Canopy – Located at SIT Campus Heart, Block W3 Level 2. Another food court option providing healthy meal selections. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/9ZpZ5o2kY4Q2QvKZA)

Partner Location:
10. The Culinary Institute of America (CIA) Singapore – Located within Temasek Polytechnic, 21 Tampines Avenue 1, Singapore 529757. This is the only SIT program still based outside Punggol. [Map Link](https://maps.app.goo.gl/7g1D8Q3NqgqXv4u89)

title: SIT lost card
text: What should i do if i lost my card? Undergraduate Concession Card Full-time matriculated students can apply for the Undergraduate Concession Card. Concession Card holders are entitled to purchase monthly concession passes to enjoy unlimited travel on basic buses and train services. Application eligibility Full-time matriculated undergraduates are eligible to apply for the Undergraduate Concession Card.Application procedure – For matriculated students Students can apply for their card here or at any SimplyGo Ticketing Service Centres (TSC). Refer to SimplyGo website for SimplyGo TSC's operating hours. More details on documents required, application fee and activation fee can be found on the SimplyGo website.Important note: Students are advised to contact SimplyGo Hotline at 1800-CALL ONE (1800-2255 663) to check your eligibility status before proceeding to any SimplyGo TSC for card application. For students who have lost your Undergraduate Concession Car. Students can contact SimplyGo hotline at 1800-2255663 and provide their personal ID number to make a loss report.More details on the Lost Card Replacement procedure can be found on the SimplyGo website. Extension of Undergraduate Concession Card Students who need to extend their course of study at SIT may apply for an extension of the concessionary eligibility period for the Undergraduate Concession Card. Please send the request via email to Registrar@SingaporeTech.edu.sg at least 10 working days before the expiry date of your existing concessionary eligibility period, with the following details: Name as stated on your Undergraduate Concession Card Expected date (DD/MM/YY) or period for completion of your course Reason for extension of course of studyThe processing time for the extension by SimplyGo is about 7 to 10 working days. Students will be notified via email to activate the extension for your concessionary eligibility period.
