title: faq_AFA
text:
Can I accept more than 1 offer and later decide which university to matriculate with?
As the Joint Acceptance Exercise portal is the same across the six local universities, you may be offered a course of study in each of the six local universities. The Joint Acceptance Exercise portal will reflect the place(s) that have been offered to you. You are only allowed to accept one offer from one of the six universities if you have been offered by more than one university. How many offers I can accept out of 5?
You can accept only one offer. When will I be informed of the outcome of my application?
SIT’s admission interviews are conducted from February to May. Most applicants will receive the outcome of their applications from April to June. How do I apply to SIT?
You can apply to SIT through the SIT Admissions Portal. The application period is from mid-January to mid-March each year. Refer to the undergraduate application guide (https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate) for more details Are there any module exemptions for SIT graduates with relevant bachelor degrees?
Yes, there are module exemptions for SIT graduates with relevant bachelor degrees. Upon completion of the ELP, participants admitted to the SIT degree programme may be granted credit exemption for certain modules undertaken during the ELP at the discretion of the University. What degree will be conferred on me at the end of the programme?
SIT offers degree programmes with and without Honours depending on individual programmes. Learn more about each programme on our [Degree Programme](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/undergraduate-programmes) page. Will I get any module exemptions for the SIT Degree Programmes?
Yes, you will get module exemptions for the SIT Degree Programmes. Module exemptions may be granted for the first-year modules on a module-by-module basis subject to evaluation of your diploma and grade in related modules. Exemptions may also be considered for relevant professional or industrial certifications. are there any module exemption for SIT graduates with postgraduate degrees?
There are no module exemptions for postgraduate programmes at SIT. Can you provide information on the number of intakes for undergraduate programs at SIT each year?
Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) has one undergraduate intake each year. Applications can be made on the SIT Application Portal during the annual admissions exercise held between January to March each year. is there any student care team? how can I reach to them?
 You may email DI@singaporetech.edu.sg for more information. Or you may visit https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/contact-us. What will happen once I have fully utilised Tuition Grant for undergraduate degree?
If you have already fully utilised your Tuition Grant for degree programmes and were conferred degree qualifications, you will have to pay non-subsidised fees for the entire duration of your new programme. If I miss the acceptance deadline, can I contact the university directly to accept my offer?
If you have not accepted our offer and the deadline stated in the electronic offer letter has passed, the offer will be deemed as lapsed. All acceptances must be completed on the Joint Acceptance Exercise portal before the cut-off date. My appeal is unsuccessful. Can I re-appeal?
The Admissions Committee would have assessed your appeal holistically, taking into consideration both your academic and non-academic merit. Therefore, the result of the appeal exercise is final. You may wish to apply again and be assessed afresh in the next admissions exercise. 

Prior to submitting your next application, you are encouraged to improve your relative standing by acquiring relevant work experience. You are also encouraged to pursue other endeavours that will help you better understand the profession and industry associated with the degree programme that you are applying for. My appeal is successful, but I have now changed my mind. Can I enrol for the first programme that I was offered?
If you wish to retain your place in the previously accepted programme, you should not accept the appeal offer via the application portal. We will refer to your original acceptance during the main admissions window. My application is unsuccessful. How do I submit an appeal?
You can submit an appeal online through the SIT Application Portal during the appeal period which is usually from late May to early June. You will only be allowed to submit an appeal for one programme.

Please include the following in your appeal:
- Reason for appealing in approximately 200 words.
- Supporting documents (in a single file, not exceeding 5 MB).

The outcome of online appeals will be released by the end of June. How do I accept my admission offer?
Successful applicants are required to accept the admission offer online via the [Joint Acceptance Exercise portal]https://www.jointacceptance.edu.sg/ by the deadline stated in your electronic offer letter. Can I change my mind after accepting a particular offer?
If you wish to amend your decision after submitting your online acceptance, please log in to the Joint Acceptance Exercise portal before the cut-off date. You may submit multiple changes. The last change received before the acceptance deadline (in late May) will be considered. How does the video assessment work? Will there be instructions to guide me?
Video assessments will be recorded and submitted through SIT’s video assessment platform. Shortlisted applicants will receive instructions for their video assessments via email.

You may be assigned more than one video assessment if you are shortlisted for more than one programme. Please complete each assessment if you wish to be considered for the respective programmes that you are shortlisted for.

The order of programme choices in your application will be taken into consideration in the final offer. How can I check my application status?
You may check your application status on the [SIT Application Portal](https://admission.singaporetech.edu.sg/). Will admission interviews be conducted face-to-face on campus? Is it possible to have an interview through video call instead?
Some admissions interviews will be conducted face-to-face on campus.

Should the situation not allow for on-site interviews, we will be able to assess shortlisted applicants through alternative means such as video assessments and online (Zoom) interviews. 

Specific instructions will be shared with shortlisted applicants in their interview invites. Must all applicants go through an interview?
Only shortlisted candidates will be invited for an admissions assessment. This can be in varying formats including online and face-to-face interviews. Can I pay my application fee online? / How do I pay for my fees?
You can pay for your application fee through the SIT Application Portal. Online payment can be made through PayNow, Credit or Debit Card. I made a duplicate payment for my application fee. How can I get a refund?
Please email StudentFinance@SingaporeTech.edu.sg and provide the following information: 
- Your application number 
- Details and proof of your duplicate payment Is there an application fee for postgraduate programmes?
An application fee of $16.35 (including GST) will be required. The payment needs to be made online and the deadline to make payment is 1 week after the application window closes. Please note that this application fee is not refundable. Is there an application form that I can download, complete manually and submit?
We do not have any physical application forms. All applications must be submitted online via the [SIT Application Portal](https://admission.singaporetech.edu.sg/). How do I update my particulars or rectify a mistake if I have already submitted my application?
You can send an email to [Adm@SingaporeTech.edu.sg](mailto:Adm@SingaporeTech.edu.sg) with the following information:
- Your full name and NRIC number 
- Your application number 
- Details of the correction required 

We will update your application and notify you accordingly. How do I apply to read a postgraduate programme?
You may submit your application and supporting documents online at our [postgraduate portal](https://admission-postgrad.singaporetech.edu.sg/) during the admission period.

For applicants applying for Postgraduate Programmes by Research, please reach out to us via email at PPR@SingaporeTech.edu.sg prior to submitting your application online.

Refer to the [Postgraduate Application Guide](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/sites/default/files/PostgraduateApplicationUserGuide1.pdf) for the steps to submit an online application. How many degree programme choices can I indicate in my online application?
You may indicate up to five degree programme choices, in order of your preference. How do I reset my password for the SIT Application Portal?
To reset your password, visit the [SIT Application Portal](https://admission.singaporetech.edu.sg/) and click the ‘Forgot Your Password’ button. A new password will be sent to you registered email. Are there overseas opportunities?
Yes! SIT students do have the opportunity to go on an overseas exchange as part of their studies.

Students pursuing joint or Overseas University (OU) undergraduate programmes will have the opportunity to attend an Overseas Immersion Programme (OIP) at the home campus of our OU partners. 

Students pursuing both SIT and joint degree programmes will have the opportunity to participate in the Overseas Integrated Work Study Programme (OIWSP) and other optional programmes, such as the Overseas Exposure Programme (OEP).

Find out more about the [Global Experience](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/life-at-sit/global-experience) that SIT offers. What is the application process and what are the important dates to note?
1. Apply via the SIT online application portal at SingaporeTech.edu.sg
2. Shortlisted applicants are invited for online admissions interviews
3. Check final application outcome via email notification from SIT or via the SIT online application portal
4. Successful applicants are required to accept their offer via the Joint Acceptance Platform or by completing an acceptance form (adhere to instructions in the e-offer letter)
5. Successful applicants who have accepted their offer will receive a pre-matriculation package via email, and are required to complete the stipulated procedures by the deadline

Please visit our [Undergraduate Admissions](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate#important-dates) page to view important dates and deadlines. Can I choose to forego the honours award and only complete the degree programme without honours?
Undergraduate students will be required to complete the whole course of study. You will be awarded a degree classification based on your GPA grades. More details can be found here  (https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate/requirements/degree-classification-singapore-institute-technology) Will I graduate with an honours degree if I am enrolled in a direct honours programme?
You will be awarded an honours degree if you achieve a Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) of 3.0 and above.

Refer to our [Degree Classification](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate/requirements/degree-classification-singapore-institute-technology) for more details. How does SIT select its overseas university partners?
We work closely with the Ministry of Education (MOE), as well as with other relevant government agencies, to identify the programme areas that support growth sectors of our economy. 

Thereafter, we identify suitable university partners that provide high-quality training in these areas, have good academic standing and strong industry reputation, and are committed to provide a quality education for our students. Are SIT degrees recognised by employers?

Singapore Institute of Technology graduates are recognised and highly sought-after by various sectors of the industry, evident in the annual Joint Autonomous Universities Graduate Employment Survey (JAUGES) conducted for the Class of 2024. The survey revealed that close to 9 in 10 (87%) SIT graduates in the labour force were employed within six months of graduation, with nearly 8 in 10 (79%) securing full-time permanent roles. https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/news/employers-value-sit-graduates-demand-stays-healthy-and-salaries-rise-driven-industry-relevant How long do the degree programmes last at SIT?
The duration of the degree programmes at SIT varies depending on the programme. 
Learn more on our [Degree Programme](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/undergraduate-programmes) page. What degree programmes does SIT offer?
Here’s a list of our current offerings on our [Degree Programme](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/undergraduate-programmes) page. Zero in on each programme by filtering them according to your fields of interest. Does SIT offer part-time studies for undergraduate degree programmes?
We do not offer part-time studies for our undergraduate programmes. However, there are [work-study options](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/applied-learning/skillsfuture-work-study-degree/in-employment-applicants) for selected programmes that you can consider. How different are SIT’s programmes compared to other autonomous universities?
SIT is an autonomous university in Singapore that offers industry-focused degree programmes. SIT's programmes are designed in consultation with industry leaders and feature industry-relevant curricula. SIT also has a strong emphasis on applied learning, with students gaining real-world experience through work attachments and projects with industry partners. This prepares SIT graduates to be work-ready and has resulted in strong employment outcomes, with more than 9 in 10 graduates employed within six months of graduation. I am currently pursuing a degree in another university. Can I transfer my credits to SIT or be given credit exemption?
SIT currently does not have any arrangement for direct transfers from other universities. 

To enrol in our programmes, you will need to submit a formal application during our admission period. Your application will be assessed alongside other applicants. You can apply for transfer of credits after matriculation. Where will the SIT degree programmes be taught?
For the full list of programme venues, please refer to the individual programme information at SIT website: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/undergraduate-programmes For programmes that involve an overseas university partner, which university confers the degree?
Our overseas university (OU) partners are involved in delivering joint programmes as well as OU programmes. Joint degree programmes are conferred by both SIT as well as our OU partners, whereas OU programmes are conferred by our OU partners only. Can I retain my place in the reserved programme (as a Returning National Serviceman) if I am successful in applying for a different programme in a subsequent admissions exercise?
Yes, you can retain your place in the reserved programme if you are successful in applying for a different programme in a subsequent admissions exercise. However, all requests are reviewed on a case-by-case basis. What are the requirements to apply to SIT?
The requirements to apply to SIT vary depending on the programme you are applying for.  For more information, please visit the SIT website. Are A-Level applicants able to apply for the degree programmes offered by SIT?
Yes. A-Level applicants may apply for all SIT degree programmes (with the exception of SIT-UofG Nursing). Do note the respective programme-specific admission requirements stated on the website at https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate/requirements/a-levels. Is there an age limit for postgraduate studies?
There is no age limit for postgraduate students. What are the admissions’ criteria?
SIT adopts an aptitude-based approach in assessing applicants for admission. Applicants are evaluated on their academic qualifications, co-curricular activity records and work experience. Shortlisted applicants will be invited for an admission interview. Please visit https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate/requirements for the specific criterias. Will I be considered for admission if I failed General Paper?
Yes, your application will be considered and assessed holistically based on both your academic and non-academic merit. Notwithstanding, selection is based on relative merit and subject to competition. I am a polytechnic student currently in my final semester. Can I apply for admission with my first five semesters' results?
Yes, if you are in the final semester of your diploma programme, you can apply for admission with your first five semesters’ results. Please note that you will need to submit your final semester results upon receiving them to complete your application. I completed my diploma through part-time studies. Am I eligible to apply?
Applicants to SIT are required to present a local full-time polytechnic diploma, GCE A-Level certificate, or equivalent Year 12 qualifications. Part-time diplomas will be reviewed on a case-by-case basis and may be accepted alongside relevant work experience and other activities that demonstrate interest in the area of study. I will be sitting for my IB Diploma examinations in May. Can I apply for admission using forecast results?
Yes, Singapore Institute of Technology accepts IB Diploma examinations. You must obtain a minimum grade of five for at least two HL and one SL subjects and the IB Diploma while satisfying the Mother Tongue Language (MTL) requirements. Can I qualify for admission if I have not fulfilled the Mother Tongue Language (MTL) requirement?
You may still apply for admission even if you have not fulfilled the MTL requirement. Your application will be reviewed without prejudice.

If you are accepted, you will be required to (i) attain any of the stipulated MTL requirements as a private candidate, or (ii) attend equivalent courses conducted by pre-approved language schools, before being allowed to graduate. I have a diploma from a local polytechnic but do not have a GCE O-Level certificate. Can I apply for admission?
Yes, you can apply for admission. You will also be required to provide details of your secondary and post-secondary qualifications, such as Year 10, Higher Nitec, Nitec, or Polytechnic Foundation Programme.

If you have gone through the Integrated Programme (IP) and did not take the GCE O-Level exams, please provide your Secondary 4 (Year 10) or equivalent standard examination results. What is the minimum GPA required for a local polytechnic diploma holder to get into SIT?
SIT adopts a holistic approach in assessing polytechnic graduates for admission by considering both academic and non-academic merit. While there is no fixed minimum GPA to secure admission into SIT degree programmes, the competition is keen every year.

Applicants are encouraged to refer to our [Indicative Grade Profile](https://singaporetech.edu.sg/files/igp) based on the last admissions exercise to assess their chances of being offered a place to read a specific degree programme. What are the requirements for SIT’s postgraduate programmes?
To be eligible for Engineering degrees, candidates must have completed a Bachelor's degree in Engineering or possess equivalent qualifications and at least one year of relevant post-bachelor's work experience. For postgraduate programmes by research, interested applicants are required to complete the Research Collaboration Agreement (RCA) before applying for admission. Will SIT consider part-time work experience as part of my total work experience during the application process?
Yes, part-time work experience may be considered as valid work experience if it is substantial and relevant to the programme of choice in the holistic review for admission. Can I apply if I am a non-Singapore citizen?
We welcome all applicants, regardless of their citizenship, to apply for our programmes. What is the minimum Cut-Off Point for SIT?
SIT practises an aptitude-based admissions selection process where applicants are not only assessed based on their academic results; we will also review their co-curricular interests, work/internship experiences, portfolios/ essays (if applicable) and interview performances, just to name a few. As such, there is no minimum cut-off point and we strongly encourage all who meet our admission requirements, and who are interested in respective programmes to apply.

Even so, selection for admissions is a competitive process for a limited number of places, and it is based on relative merit (both academically and non-academically). To keep prospective students informed of SIT's respective programmes and their chances of gaining acceptance into SIT, we have published the SIT [Indicative Grade Profile](https://singaporetech.edu.sg/files/igp) as a guide. How many undergraduate intakes does SIT have each year?
SIT has one undergraduate intake each year. Applications can be made on our Application Portal during our annual admissions exercise held between January to March each year. When do the classes commence for SIT degree programmes?
Classes for SIT degree programmes typically commence in September. What are SIT’s tuition fees like?
SIT’s tuition fees for undergraduate programmes are cohort-based. Students pay a fixed fee throughout their candidature, based on their year of intake.

National Servicemen who defer the commencement of their studies will continue to pay the prevailing tuition fees for the year they accepted SIT’s offer of enrolment.

Please refer to [https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/tuition-fees](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/tuition-fees) for information on Tuition Fees. What are the miscellaneous fees and what do they cover?
The miscellaneous fees are part of the cost of providing insurance, student programmes, and activities that are not covered by the tuition fee and grant. The fee is payable at the beginning of each trimester and is non-refundable.

For more information, please visit SIT [miscellaneous fees](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate/tuition-fees#miscellaneous-fees) page. I am facing issues regarding payment, what can I do?
If you have encountered any issues, you may reach out to us at Adm@SingaporeTech.edu.sg citing your full name and application ID, as well as attaching any supporting screenshots. How can I find out about SIT’s postgraduate tuition fees?
Postgraduate tuition fees are not fixed throughout the student’s entire candidature and are subject to yearly revision. More details are available on [the Postgraduate Tuition Fees](https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/postgraduate/tuition-fees) page. How much is the application fee and when is the deadline?
An application fee of $16.35 (including GST) will be required. The payment needs to be made online and the deadline to make payment is 1 week after the application window closes. What are the Financial Assistance Schemes available to SIT students? / I need help with fees / I need help with tuition fees.
At SIT, we are committed to providing access to a quality education without letting any financial difficulties get in the way. There are various financial assistance schemes available to help with educational expenses.
For more information, please refer to:
Financial Aid | Singapore Institute of Technology (singaporetech.edu.sg) I have some outstanding tuition fees. Can I proceed to apply for a postgraduate programme and clear my outstanding fees after?
Please make settlement for any outstanding fees before applying for a postgraduate programme. When can I apply for Financial Aids?
Matriculating students may submit their applications for the Financial Assistance according to the application instructions and deadlines as stated in the pre-matriculation package sent via email.  

Current students may submit their Financial Assistance applications during the same application periods.

Information on the application periods and submission instructions can be found in the Loans, Bursaries & other Assistance Schemes pages. What are the Scholarships available to SIT students?
SIT is pleased to offer a range of scholarships that will equip SITizens with the necessary resources and opportunities to fulfil their full potential.
These scholarships are generally awarded based on merit and duration ranges from one academic year to the full duration of the degree programme.

For more information, please refer to:
Scholarships | Singapore Institute of Technology (singaporetech.edu.sg) Where is the SIT campus located?
SIT Punggol Campus is located at 1 Punggol Coast Road, Singapore 828608. How do I withdraw my application after I have submitted it?
If you wish to withdraw your application, please email [Adm@SingaporeTech.edu.sg](mailto:Adm@SingaporeTech.edu.sg) and indicate your: 
- Full name and NRIC number 
- Application number 
- Intention to withdraw your application  

We will update your status accordingly upon receiving your intention to withdraw. However, please note that application fees will not be refundable. What is SkillsFuture Work-Study Degree (WSDeg) or Term-In, Term-Out (TITO) for SIT Students?
They are designed to allow students to acquire generic and technical skills by increasing work-related components of their studies to help them facilitate students’ transition from the university to the workplace. I am not a Singapore citizen or Permanent Resident. Do I need to apply for a Student’s Pass?
International students who are accepted for the postgraduate programmes (coursework) must apply for a Student’s Pass. Instructions for [Student’s Pass application](https://www.ica.gov.sg/reside/STP/apply) can be found on the Immigration & Checkpoints Authority website.

If you are an Employment Pass holder and do not intend to relinquish it, please inform us by sending an email to Adm.Postgrad@SingaporeTech.edu.sg. How does a student stand to benefit if he/she is accepted into WSDeg or TITO?
Students will be given a stronger grounding of theory in real work environments, equipping them to become job-ready employees right upon graduation.
While applying what they have learnt in school at work, they will also be supported by their companies. This may include, but is not restricted to, tuition fee sponsorship, stipends, and sign-on bonuses.
Students will gain the opportunity to learn more about the company’s work culture.
They may be offered career opportunities with the partner company upon graduation.
Acceptance into the SkillsFuture WSDeg (Term-In/Term-Out model) is an effective career jump-start for students as they stand to gain at least 10-16 months of accumulated relevant work experience during the course of their undergraduate studies. How is SkillsFuture Work-Study Degree programmes or TITO delivered for SIT Students?
SkillsFuture Work-Study Degree (WSDeg) or Term-In, Term-Out (TITO) for SIT students is delivered in a 'Term-In Term-Out' (TITO) model. Students will spend between one to twelve months in the university and at the workplace alternately during their undergraduate studies. How different is the structure of the WSDeg or TITO programme, compared to a full-time degree programme at SIT?
The structure for the programmes, following the Term-In/Term-Out model, remains largely similar to full-time degree programmes at SIT. The full-time programmes run on a five-day study structure with stipulated periods set aside for on-the-job training (OJT). I am an undergraduate student from another Autonomous University, will I still be eligible for a Tuition Grant if I transfer to another full-time undergraduate degree programme in SIT? 
If are you transferring from another Autonomous University (ie. NTU, NUS, SMU, SUSS, SUTD) to a full-time SIT undergraduate degree programme and have an existing Tuition Grant from your current University, you will still be eligible for the Tuition Grant. 

Do note that it will be computed based on a pro-rated method to determine the remaining semesters or credits which qualify for the Tuition Grant. 

For example, if you have taken 4 out of 8 semesters of Tuition Grant from another Autonomous University, you will be eligible for a Tuition Grant which is 50% (4/8 semesters) of the total credits to complete the new full-time undergraduate degree programme in SIT. Who is eligible to apply for WSDeg or TITO?
Year 1 and/or Year 2* SIT students in the following programmes will be eligible to apply for SkillsFuture WSDeg, delivered in the Term-In/Term-Out model:

Bachelor of Engineering in Information and Communications Technology (Information Security)
Bachelor of Engineering in Information and Communications Technology (Software Engineering) 
Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Computer Engineering
Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Robotics Systems
Bachelor of Science in Applied Computing (Fintech)
* to ICT (IS & SE) programmes only. What will happen once I have fully utilised all my MOE Tuition Grant and SIT subsidies? 
In the event that you have fully utilised all MOE Tuition Grant and SIT subsidies, you will be charged non-subsidised tuition fees. Is there anything I should do before applying for a new programme?
Before applying for a new programme, you should plan your admission strategy by noting important dates and preparing in advance. You should also select your programme carefully and craft a well-rounded application. The application period is from mid-January to mid-March each year. Can I submit my application for a change of programme after the application window has closed?
You cannot submit your application for a change of programme after the application window has closed. However, current SIT students can apply for a change of programme through the Student Intranet during the stipulated period for “Change of Programme” Is there an application fee for my change of programme?
You will be required to pay a sum of $16.35 for the application to change your programme. Does an application for a change of programme guarantee me a place in the new programme?
Applying for a change of programme DOES NOT guarantee you a place in the new programme. Submitting your change of programme application does not constitute the approval of your application into the new programme. All transfers will be assessed on a case-by-case basis and you may be required to undergo an interview and/or written test to assess your suitability for the new programme Does SIT offer any scholarships? 
SIT offers a range of scholarships that will equip SITizens with the necessary resources and opportunities to fulfil their full potential. Fore more information, please visit https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/scholarships When is the term break for SIT Degree Programmes?
You may refer to the academic calendar https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate/academic-calendar-sit-and-joint-programmes for more details. What kind of clubs and teams are there in SIT?
We offer a vibrant array of clubs and teams, spanning across diverse categories to cater to various interests and developmental opportunities:

Arts and Culture Clubs
Global Citizenship
Industry Chapters
Special Interest Clubs
Sports Clubs
Competitive Varsity Sports Teams
To explore our extensive range of clubs and teams, head over to our corporate site link. Have any questions or seeking further information? Reach out to us at OSE@Singaporetech.edu.sg. We're here to help and guide you toward finding the perfect fit for your interests and passions! What if I can't find a club that I am interested in?
 If you're enthusiastic about starting your own club here at SIT, we're all ears and ready to support your initiative. Drop us a note at OSE@Singaporetech.edu.sg to kickstart the conversation. Let's work together to transform your club idea into an exciting reality here at SIT! Do you have a Student Government/Union in SIT?
In SIT, Student Management Committees represent the respective programme cohorts and act as the voice of the students and a bridge between the university and the student body. These committees also regularly organise activities that promote bonding amongst students within and beyond the academic programmes that they represent.

SMCs organise various engagement sessions throughout the year for students within and beyond their degree programmes. Some of these engagement sessions include Annual General Meetings, talks by industry partners, and community events such as welfare pack distribution, etc.  ​

​SMCs also co-organise Day Zero (an academic induction) with their faculty and Office of SITizen Experience to welcome and induct freshmen into SIT. ​

​Some other interesting events planned by SMCs include outreach events to polytechnic students and cluster days to showcase their degree programme e.g. Engineering Day. What kind of training will student leaders go through? 
The Office of SITizen Experience provides a robust suite of training and engagement events which prepare our student leaders to be future ready and leaders of tomorrow. Some areas of development include communications skills, conflict resolution and managing team dynamics. What kind of leadership opportunities are available in SIT?
The Office of SITizen Experience is always keen to work with students who are interested to take up leadership opportunities. We offer a range of choices, from being an SMC, club or project leader to working as a Diversity & Inclusion Advocate, being a Team captain to a SITizen Ambassador. 

Additionally, we provide a robust suite of training for our student leaders, including the Future Ready Leadership Programme (FRLP) which equips student leaders with the tools needed to understand vital leadership concepts, develop their facilitation techniques, and excel in both communication and teamwork within diverse settings

Keen to find out? Reach out to us at OSE@SingaporeTech.edu.sg What facilities are available to SIT Students?
SIT offers an array of modern facilities to provide students with comfort and convenience in their journey with us. What kind of counselling support is available to SIT students?
Yes, there is counselling support available to SIT students. Counselling is confidential and free for all students. Students can arrange to speak to a counsellor in person at Office of SITizen Experience, SIT Punggol Campus, E6 Level 2M, or email SITCounselling@SingaporeTech.edu.sg. What support does SIT provide for Special Education Needs?
The Office of SITizen Experience is committed to providing all students with the opportunity to participate in the rich and diverse life in SIT. The Student Care Team assists students with with special needs in attaining equal access to academic programmes, extracurricular activities, and facilities on campus. The Student Care team works with faculty and students to arrange appropriate academic support for students with special needs. Students who like to request for support or have any questions about such services can contact our Student Care Officer or our Student Care Admin Support. For more information, please email SITCounselling@singaporetech.edu.sg.

title: faq_CSM
text:
Can I apply for the degree programme at any time?
You may apply for the degree programme during one of three yearly admission cycles. How does stacking to a degree work?
CSM degree programmes equip you with a strong technical foundation and real-world experience to meet industry standards. Each micro-credential earns you a specialist certificate which you can stack towards a full degree. They are broken down into micro-credentials, allowing you to master skills that matter most to you, when you want to. How long does it take to complete one micro-credential?
It takes one trimester (3+ months) to complete a micro-credential. Do I need to take micro-credentials in a particular order?
You are not required to take micro-credentials in a prescribed order. However, SIT will state the recommended baseline competencies that an individual should have. If I already possess industry certifications or relevant work experience, will these be taken into account during the application process?
Yes. In addition to SIT’s micro-credentials, your application will also take into account other prior learning such as industry certifications or competencies acquired from work, providing you with credit or exemptions. Is there a time frame to complete the degree?
There is no specific time frame to complete the degree. However, SIT recommends completing one micro-credential per trimester to graduate within three years. If you need more flexibility, you have up to five years to complete the degree. Are all micro-credentials subsidised by SkillsFuture Singapore?
The list of micro-credentials subsidised by SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) will be progressively updated.  If you are planning to stack to a degree, SSG funding is only available for up to six micro-credentials (i.e. 120 credits). Are there any interviews or additional assessments as part of the admission process?
Yes, there will be interviews and additional assessments such as the Academic Aptitude Assessment. What is the structure of micro-credentials?
Micro-credentials are largely taught asynchronously, which gives you the flexibility to learn whenever and wherever you choose. While each micro-credential combines online and in-person delivery, the learning activities vary for each micro-credential. Am I guaranteed a place in the micro-credential upon registration?
Acceptance into the micro-credential will be determined by the Teaching Team and/or if you fulfil the assumed prior knowledge (if any) stated in each respective micro-credential’s webpage. Places are allocated on a first-come, first-served basis. Can I enrol in multiple micro-credentials at the same time?
It is not advisable to enrol in more than one micro-credential at any one time given the associated commitment and workload. How will I be notified about the status of my micro-credential application?
You will receive an email notification from SITLEARN Professional Development. What is the role of the success coach in the micro-credential?
Success coaches will work with you to develop a personalised learning plan. They will provide you with a wide range of resources and support services to help you navigate your learning journey. What is the tuition fees for CSM?
You can refer to the tuition fees for CSM here https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/admissions/undergraduate/csm-tuition-fees

title: faq_add
text:
What is applied learning?
Applied Learning combines academic knowledge and skills with real-world applications in the industry and community.  Click on this link to learn more: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/why-sit/applied-learning What is STLA? What does STLA do?
STLA is the SIT Teaching & Learning Academy. STLA supports the implementation of positive teaching, learning, and assessment practices to realise SIT’s educational vision. Click here to learn more: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/colead/ What is AdventureLEARN?
AdventureLEARN is a game-based platform designed for SIT freshmen to develop important soft skills as they make the transition to university. Click here to learn more: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/colead/collaborations/student-resources/adventureLEARN What is Quests?
Quests are self-paced online courses on Math, Physics, and Chemistry designed with Adaptive Learning methodologies for Freshmen to revise Pre-University fundamentals before they start Year 1 studies. Click here to learn more: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/colead/collaborations/student-resources/quests What is Learning to Learn Better (LTLB)?
Learning to Learn Better is a programme for students to strengthen their learning skills and strategies. Click here to learn more: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/colead/collaborations/student-resources/learning-to-learn-better What is Teaching Excellence Award (TEA)?
Teaching Excellence Award (TEA) is an award that recognises and rewards academic staff who have made a significant contribution to learning in SIT. SIT staff, existing students and alumni (who graduated within 1 year of the degree programme) are welcome to submit a nomination. Click here to learn more: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/colead/grants-and-awards/teaching-awards When is the next Applied Learning Conference?
Applied Learning Conference is a biennial conference organised by SIT to propel conversations and collaborations on Applied Learning. You may refer to this link to find out when is the next conference: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/colead/events/applied-learning-conference What is the definition of a SITizen? / What are SITizens? / Who are called SITizens? 
 The day you join SIT as a learner, you become a SITizen. And we have a saying: Once a SITizen, always a SITizen. We believe that learning is for life. Even after you graduate, you will be a SITizen for life. What is Student Developer Award (SDA)?
Student Developer Award (SDA) is an award that recognises and celebrates non-academic members of the SIT community who have positively contributed to the learning experiences of our students. SIT staff, existing students and alumni (who graduated within 1 year of the degree programme) are welcome to submit a nomination. Click here to learn more: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/colead/grants-and-awards/student-developer-awards Who are SIT Overseas University Partners?
SIT partners with reputable overseas universities from the United States, Europe, and Australasia. This partnership brings a worldwide perspective to our practice-oriented learning, all while keeping us closely connected to the industry. Ready to explore more about these amazing Overseas University Partners? Check them out here. Trust me, it's a gateway to a world of incredible collaborations!(https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/about/overseas-university-partners).

title: faq_campus
text:
What are the transport options to get to Punggol Campus?
As part of our 'Campus-in-the-park' vision aligning with Punggol Digital District's eco-commuting policy, we're moving towards a car-lite district with fewer parking lots and limited season parking. To support eco-friendly commuting, we strongly encourage students and staff to opt for public transportation.

Public Transport: Punggol Campus is served by both public bus and rail options.

MRT: Punggol Coast MRT Station is located at the doorstep of the campus. Please use Exit 1.

Bus: Punggol Coast Bus Interchange, situated adjacent to the campus, is served by bus services 34 and 117/117M. Alternatively, services 84W and 84G from the Punggol Bus Interchange also provide direct access to the campus.

LRT: Teck Lee LRT station is within walking distance of Punggol Campus. Pedestrians can walk via the covered linkway and through Campus Boulevard.

Cars: Punggol Campus is within Punggol Digital District (PDD), a designated car-lite district. Visitors are encouraged to use public transport as parking spaces and season parking in PDD are limited. Public parking facilities are available in the vicinity.

Bicycles and Personal Mobility Devices: Dedicated parking spaces and End-of-Trip (EOT) facilities, including lockers and toilets with shower facilities, are strategically located across the campus.
Reference Link: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/about/punggol-campus#:~:text=Our%20%27Campus%2Din,across%20the%20campus. Will there be internal shuttle bus services?
Punggol Campus is designed for exploration on foot, featuring designated walkways and link bridges that facilitate seamless access between the two plots: SIT Campus Court and SIT Campus Heart.
Reference Link: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/about/punggol-campus#:~:text=Is%20there%20an%20internal%20shuttle%20bus%20service%20within%20Punggol%20Campus%3F Will chartered bus services be available from other parts of Singapore to the Punggol Campus?
Given its connectivity and proximity to the Punggol Coast MRT station and bus interchange, there won't be any chartered bus services provided to the Punggol Campus. The public transportation options nearby aim to facilitate easy access for all students and staff commuting to the campus.
Reference Link: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/about/punggol-campus#:~:text=Are%20there%20chartered%20bus%20services%20available%20from%20other%20parts%20of%20Singapore%20to%20Punggol%20Campus%3F What will happen to SIT@Dover and SIT@Poly Buildings?
SIT's existing campuses will gradually be returned to the respective authorities when they're no longer needed. The new centralised campus in Punggol will host all programmes except those offered by The Culinary Institute of America (CIA). CIA will continue to operate from its current location at Temasek Polytechnic.
Reference Link: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/about/punggol-campus#:~:text=What%20will%20happen%20to%20SIT%40Poly%20Buildings%3F Is the Punggol Campus accessible to the public?
The design of the Punggol Campus aims to foster integration among those sited within the Punggol Digital District and the wider Punggol community. As part of this vision, floors from the Campus Boulevard (Level 2) and below will be accessible to members of the public. This accessibility promotes engagement and collaboration between SIT and the broader community, enhancing the overall exchange of ideas and opportunities.
Reference Link: https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/about/punggol-campus#:~:text=Is%20the%20Punggol%20Campus%20accessible%20to%20the%20public%3F How were the needs and expectations of students and staff taken into consideration when designing the new campus?
 The journey towards creating our campus at Punggol was a collaborative effort from the get-go. From 2016 to 2019, we conducted numerous visioning workshops, ideation sessions, brainstorming sessions, and interviews with over 500 student and staff representatives. This inclusive approach helped shape the SIT campus concept plan and design requirements.

The final design underwent meticulous review and revision, a process that involved the invaluable feedback and input from key stakeholders of the University. This thorough and inclusive process ensured that the approved design truly reflects the collective vision and needs of our SIT community.

SIT students and staff will have priority access to the facilities and amenities in our Punggol Campus. Requests by external parties will be managed on a case-by-case basis. There are several spaces dedicated just for students. These areas are specially designed to support your study sessions and project work. From cozy study nooks to collaborative project zones, we've got you covered.

Specific zones across the campus have been geared up to support round-the-clock study activities during those peak periods in the academic calendar. text:
What is Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours about?
The Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours, a comprehensive three-year degree crafted in collaboration with industry leaders, accreditation bodies, and external faculty experts. This program is designed to refine not only your academic knowledge but also your critical and analytical skills, preparing you for a successful career in accounting within commerce, industry, government, or public practice.

As a graduate, you'll emerge as a specialist equipped with a deep skill set tailored for the dynamic accounting sector. Explore more about the program and its benefits here. We're here to support your journey toward a rewarding future in accountancy! What are the career options for Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours?
Explore a variety of exciting career paths such as becoming a Chartered Accountant/Certified Practising Accountant, Management and Taxation Consultant, Auditor, Financial and Risk Analyst, Financial Controller, Fraud Investigator, Forensic Accountant, or even venturing into entrepreneurship! There are so many possibilities waiting for you to discover. What are the entry requirements for Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours?
If you have a diploma from any of the five local polytechnics, A-level/IB Diploma qualifications, or equivalent Year 12 qualifications, you're more than welcome to apply for the Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours. We're excited to consider applicants from diverse educational backgrounds! Will there be module exemptions if I hold a related qualification?
If you have a background in accounting, such as holding a Diploma in Accountancy/Accounting, you're eligible to apply for exemptions for up to two trimesters from the first year. Your prior knowledge will play a part in making your academic journey smoother! What is unique about the Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours programme?
One of the features is the 8-month Integrated Work Study Programme (IWSP), where you'll dive into areas like auditing, business consulting, data analytics, risk management, and taxation. During IWSP, you'll tackle real industry problems in the Applied Analytics Capstone module using cutting-edge data analytics tools. In your final year, the options are yours to choose from—pick one of five specialisations (Applied Finance, Audit, Corporate Accounting, Management Accounting, or Tax) and delve deeper into advanced professional core knowledge. Plus, you will get a unique opportunity to experience Regional Exposure to Accounting Practice in ASEAN countries! It's a dynamic blend of learning and real-world application. Where will Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours be offered?
All classes will be held at SIT's Punggol Campus. How long are the work attachments for Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours?
Our Integrated Work Study Programme isn't your typical internship—it's an immersive eight-month experience! This program offers you the chance to dive deep into the real world of work, allowing you to apply the theories you've learned in class to actual practice. Get ready to gain invaluable real work experience that goes beyond the ordinary internship! What is the duration of study for Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours?
The duration of Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours programme is 3 years. What is Bachelor of Science with Aviation Management about?
This three-year program, the first and only of its kind offered by an autonomous university in Singapore, promises an exciting adventure. Our curriculum is crafted in collaboration with the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS) and other aviation industry experts. 

Dive into the details of the program and discover more about the fantastic opportunities right here. <https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/undergraduate-programmes/air-transport-management> Is the Bachelor of Accountancy with Honours accredited by any professional bodies?
Our programme is proudly accredited by several esteemed professional bodies, including:

- Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA)
- Chartered Accountants Australia and New Zealand (CA ANZ)
- Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) – as part of the University Affiliation Program
- Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (CIMA)
- CPA Australia
- Institute of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales (ICAEW)
- Singapore Accountancy Commission / Institute of Singapore Chartered Accountants (SAC / ISCA)
Excitingly, our programme is also part of the CFA Institute University Recognition Programme. This recognition comes as we incorporate a substantial 70% of the CFA Programme Candidate Body of Knowledge (CBOK). This not only provides our students with a strong foundation in the CBOK but also thoroughly prepares them for success in the CFA exams. What are the career options for Bachelor of Science with Honours in Aviation Management?
As the aviation sector spreads its wings, graduates can explore career paths in airports, airlines, ground handling agencies, aviation consultancy firms, regulatory bodies, business units, freight forwarders, and even tech firms. 

The sky's the limit for your future career possibilities! What are the entry requirements for Bachelor of Science with Honours in Aviation Management?
We welcome diploma holders from our local polytechnics, A-level/NUS High School Diploma/IB Diploma graduates, and those with equivalent Year 12 qualifications to apply!

The selection process is in the hands of the University, and depends largely on the number of spots available in the program. So get ready for takeoff and apply into the Bachelor of Science with Honours in Air Transport Management today! How long is the work attachments for Bachelor of Science with Honours in Aviation Management?
Our Integrated Work Study Programme isn't your typical internship—it's an immersive eight-month experience! This program offers you the chance to dive deep into the real world of work, allowing you to apply the theories you've learned in class to actual practice. Get ready to gain invaluable real work experience that goes beyond the ordinary internship! What is unique about the Bachelor of Science with Honours in Aviation Management programme?
Some cool highlights of the programme includes:

- An Industry-Relevant Curriculum, crafted hand-in-hand with CAAS and top-notch Aviation Companies.
- An Eight-Month Integrated Work Study Programme at Airlines, Airports, and Ground Handling Agencies.
- Specialised Modules like Airport Operations and Management, Airline Marketing, Airport Marketing, and Revenue Analysis and Management. 

It's all about making learning fun and practical! Where will Bachelor of Science with Honours in Aviation Management be offered?
All classes will be held at SIT's Punggol Campus. What is the duration of study for Bachelor of Science with Honours in Aviation Management?
The duration of Bachelor of Science with Honours in Air Transport Management programme is 3 years. What are the career options for Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Aircraft Systems Engineering?
Check out these awesome career paths waiting for you!

- Licensed Aircraft Engineer
- Process, Quality and Product Engineer
- Maintenance Planner
- Fleet Manager
- Technical Service/Repair Development Engineer What are the entry requirements for Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Aircraft Systems Engineering?
We welcome polytechnic diploma holders—check the list of stipulated courses here [https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/undergraduate-programmes/aircraft-systems-engineering] to see if yours qualifies.

Applicants with other engineering diplomas, A-Level/IB Diploma qualifications, or equivalent Year 12 qualifications are also encouraged to apply.

If your diploma isn’t listed, applications are considered on a case-by-case basis. Bring your unique qualifications, and we’ll explore how you can join the programme. What is Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Aircraft Systems Engineering about?
The Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Aircraft Systems Engineering is a three-year direct honours programme designed for those passionate about aircraft and hands-on engineering.

In partnership with SIA Engineering Company (SIAEC), students gain practical experience in aircraft maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO), working alongside a team that services over 80 international airlines and aerospace equipment manufacturers.

The programme combines engineering theory with applied learning, preparing graduates to make a meaningful impact in the aerospace and MRO industries. How long is the work attachments for Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Aircraft Systems Engineering?
Our Integrated Work Study Programme isn't your typical internship—it's an immersive eight-month experience at SIAEC! This program offers you the chance to dive deep into the real world of work, allowing you to apply the theories you've learned in class to actual practice. 

Get ready to gain invaluable real work experience that goes beyond the ordinary internship! What is the duration of study for Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Aircraft Systems Engineering?
The duration of Bachelor of Science with Honours in Air Transport Management programme is 3 years. Where will  Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Aircraft Systems Engineering be offered?
Starting from September 2024, classes will take place at the brand-new Punggol Campus. But don't worry, before the big move, you can still catch your classes at the current SIT@Dover location. What are the career options for Bachelor of Science  in Applied Computing with Honours?
Check out these awesome career paths waiting for you!

-Data Analyst
-Data Engineer
-Software Engineer
-DevOps Engineer What is Bachelor of Science with Honours in Applied Computing with Honours about?
The Bachelor of Science with Honours in Applied Computing (Fintech) is a three-year direct honours programme designed for those ready to explore where technology meets finance.

Developed in partnership with the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) and the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS), the programme combines computing, software engineering, and machine learning with practical insights into banking compliance, financial products, and investment management.

Gain hands-on experience and the skills needed to thrive in the fintech industry. Learn more about the programme [https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/undergraduate-programmes/applied-computing-fintech]. Will there be exemptions for related qualifications holder?
Yes, you may be eligible for module exemptions based on your diploma and academic performance in relevant modules. Exemptions are assessed on a module-by-module basis.

Professional or industry certifications may also be considered for exemptions where applicable. This process ensures that prior learning and experience are appropriately recognised. What is unique about the Bachelor of Sciences in Applied Computing with Honours programme?
Interested in how software, machine learning, and cybersecurity power the financial world? The Bachelor of Science in Applied Computing with Honours brings it all together.

Developed with the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) and industry experts, this programme equips you with the skills to excel in tech-driven finance.

Learn more about the Applied Computing programme here [https://www.singaporetech.edu.sg/undergraduate-programmes/applied-computing-fintech]. What are the entry requirements for Bachelor of Science  in Applied Computing with Honours?
We welcome applicants who are diploma holders from the five local polytechnics, A-Level or IB Diploma graduates, or holders of equivalent Year 12 qualifications.

If you’re ready to take the next step, submit your application. We value diverse talents and look forward to seeing how your experience contributes to our community. How long is the work attachments for Bachelor of Science in Applied Computing with Honours?
At SIT, we're not just about classrooms and textbooks – we're all about rolling up our sleeves and diving into the real deal! 

Get ready for the thrill of an eight-month Integrated Work Study Programme (IWSP) with a company! This is a chance for you to weave those theories into real-world wizardry, soak up industry secrets, and sprout professional skills that'll make you stand out in the crowd.

But here's the sweet part – it's not your typical internship. It's a chance to join a company and become one with their culture. Imagine, by the end of it, you're not just job-ready; you're practically a part of the workforce already!

So, why wait? Dive into the real world with IWSP and set the stage for a seamless transition into your dream career. Your future self will thank you! What is the duration of study for Bachelor of Science in Applied Computing with Honours?

Ready to dive into the world of Applied Artificial Intelligence and become the next big thing in tech? Our Bachelor of Science with Honours in Applied Artificial Intelligence isn't just a programme – it's a three-year adventure crafted for future innovators like you!

Picture this: a direct honours pathway designed to mold you into an ICT pro who doesn't just understand AI but knows how to wield it, shape it, and unleash it on the world. It's not about textbooks; it's about rolling up your sleeves and getting your hands dirty with real-world AI solutions.

Curious to unlock the secrets of this AI journey? Dive into the details of the programme [here]. Where will Bachelor of Science with Honours in Applied Computing with Honours be offered?
All classes will be held at SIT's Punggol Campus. What are the career options for Bachelor of Science in Applied Artificial Intelligence with Honours?
Check out these awesome career paths waiting for you!

-AI Engineer
-Machine Learning Engineer
-Data Engineer (Machine Learning Specialist)
-AI Application Engineer What is unique about the Bachelor of Science in Applied Artificial Intelligence with Honours programme?
Ready to deep-dive into the exciting world of Applied Artificial Intelligence? Our specialized Bachelor of Science in Applied Artificial Intelligence with Honours is not your typical programme – it's a personalised journey tailored just for you!

Core-Software Engineering: This is where you'll build the rock-solid foundation for software development, a must-have for mastering those machine learning skills. Think cloud computing, big data – the works!

Core-Machine Learning: Get ready to unravel the magic behind developing, applying, and deploying AI models. It's not just about understanding; it's about hands-on mastery!

Professional Skills: Because being an AI maestro isn't just about code – it's about expressing your genius confidently. We're talking soft skills that will make you shine in any professional setting. What are the entry requirements for  Bachelor of Science in Applied Artificial Intelligence with Honours?
If you're eyeing the Bachelor of Science in Applied Artificial Intelligence with Honours, here are the entry requirements:

- Diplomas from any of our five local polytechnics

A head's up! For A-Levels / IB Diploma, there are prerequisites - you need to have obtained a good pass in any two of the following H1/H2 or SL/HL subjects: Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Computing, or Computer Science. Students gain hands-on experience through intensive lab work and practical projects, developing the skills to excel as chemical engineering professionals.

If you're curious about other institutions and their programs, feel free to reach out to them directly. Happy exploring! Will there be a new programme to replace the joint FoodTech programme, and what will the new programme offer?


From AY2024, SIT is introducing its very own FoodTech degree programme entirely conferred by SIT! The program structure and curriculum will remain the same as the current joint degree, with a small twist—new exciting electives and no Overseas Immersion Programme. Tuition fees will be just like SIT's general degrees - it's a win-win! How different are the BSN-MSN and University of Glasgow programmes from the NUS’s nursing programme?

Currently, no university in Singapore provides an integrated PET (pre-employment training) and CET (continuing education and training) pathway for nursing graduates. Typically, nurses work for 1 to 3 years before being nominated by their employer for advanced/specialist diploma courses. 

SIT's Integrated Nursing program, in partnership with SingHealth, fills this gap by jointly developing a tailored curriculum, committing to student support, and providing sponsorships and employment for graduates. These unique features are not found in NUS's Bachelor of Science (Nursing) degree. How does the BSN-MSN programme differ from the existing Nursing programme which SIT already offers? What are the key differences? 

SIT currently collaborates with the University of Glasgow to offer a post-registration nursing program at the bachelor's level for registered nurses.

 In an effort to expand programme offerings, SIT is introducing the BSN-MSN program, targeting fresh school leavers without prior Registered Nurse (RN) training. This program aims to provide a faster and more comprehensive option for students to acquire advanced nursing skillsets relevant to the evolving health ecosystem. In contrast, the University of Glasgow joint program serves as a "top-up" degree, acknowledging prior learning at polytechnics and building on existing knowledge from nursing diploma studies, but it does not include specialty training. Is SIT the first university in Singapore to offer this specialisation in Agritech and Aquaculture?
 Yes, SIT is the first university in Singapore to offer this specialisation in Agritech and Aquaculture What if I'm interested in mechatronics and not specifically robotics? Does SIT have a programme for me?
The Robotic Systems programme share similarities with the Mechatronics Systems programme such as mechatronics and systems engineering. Alternatively, other programmes that include mechatronics-related topics in their curricula include (i) Mechanical Engineering, and (ii) Mechanical Design and Manufacturing Engineering programmes. What are some career pathways for graduates from this specialisation in Agritech and Aquaculture?
Graduates can anticipate employment opportunities across various sectors, including the government sector, aquaculture and ornamental farms, marine conservation parks, and marine research facilities or institutions, in the following positions:
•	Farm Engineer at food fish/ ornamental fish farms
•	Aquarist/ Agrotechnologist at Marine/ Agri conservation parks
•	Research Engineer at Marine/ Agri research facilities
•	Seafood/ Farm Manager at government agencies
•	Data Scientist 
•	Facility Manager in urban farms
•	Aquaculture and Agritech System Engineers What are the BSN-MSN programme’s learning objectives ?
The educational objectives of BSN-MSN Programme include:
a.	Develop nurses with advanced theoretical and clinical knowledge, attributes, and values relating to the nursing profession.
b. 	Develop ethical, professional, and competent practitioners who are able to demonstrate the health sciences attributes of adaptability, effective communication, evidence-based mindset and professionalism.
